question: What’s the difference between carrydown and breakdown?
content:
Carrydown vs. Breakdown
Breakdown
This happens when balls remove oil from the front and midlane.
What causes it
Strong covers absorbing oil
Repeated shots through the same part of the lane
High friction surfaces
What it does to ball motion
Front of the lane gets drier
Ball loses energy earlier
Hook starts sooner and becomes rounder
You may start “running out of room” because the ball checks too early
How it feels to the bowler
“My ball is hooking too early.”
“I can’t keep it out long enough.”
Carrydown
This happens when balls push oil further downlane instead of absorbing it.
What causes it
Weaker covers / low diff cores (urethane, no flare balls)
Repeated shots with low absorption equipment
What it does to ball motion
Back end becomes slicker
Ball skids longer
Hook happens later and weaker
You may miss the breakpoint or leave flat corners
How it feels to the bowler
“My ball won’t turn.”
“It keeps sliding past the spot.”
The core difference
Breakdown = oil disappears → earlier hook.
Carrydown = oil moves downlane → later hook.
